test_cases.description: Single documentary film — Cold War radio operators — European public broadcaster
test_cases.input_summary: 75-min film, 3 elderly subjects, Cold War/technology angle, two comps provided, development stage, no financing
test_cases.output_summary: 600-word pitch with logline, editorial hook citing infrastructure decommission timing, character descriptions, observational approach statement, accurate comps placement, production status block

test_cases.description: 4-part series — climate migration, Southeast Asia — SVOD platform
test_cases.input_summary: 4x45min series, access to three communities in Philippines, Thailand, Indonesia, partial financing (one development grant), seeking streaming lead partner
test_cases.output_summary: Pitch adapts tone for SVOD (global audience framing, binge structure hinted), logline anchors on individual stories not geopolitics, production status shows existing support as credibility signal

test_cases.description: Single film — intimate portrait of a midwife — national public broadcaster, no comps provided
test_cases.input_summary: 60-min film, one central character, character-driven rather than issue-driven, no comps given, broadcaster is generalist public channel
test_cases.output_summary: Claude generates two suggested comps with caveat to verify, pitch body foregrounds character intimacy as the editorial hook, avoids over-claiming social importance

notes: Fluency is the strongest dimension — prose pitch writing is a natural fit for this skill. Relevance and coherence are consistent. Actionability score reflects that the pitch document requires user personalization for specific broadcaster strands before submission.
